title: Superhuman Robot Execution
description: Extract accelerable execution strategies from slow teleoperation
summary: This project asks whether slow human teleoperation can provide the task path, then DAgger + SFT first improves stability and safety, SFT action-frequency ablations measure speed / continuity tradeoffs, and RL further optimizes speed and smoothness.
risks_decisions: Need to distinguish hardware capability from policy speed; faster timing alone may not reproduce EKA-like performance.
Contact-rich manipulation may fail under naive acceleration even if the geometric path and event order are unchanged.
The first learnable object should probably be the rate curve or action latent, not arbitrary raw actions; otherwise the project becomes generic RL.
Force / tactile can provide the safer reward, but real-robot deployment still needs guardrails for force spikes, collision, and hardware stress.
SFT action sampling frequency may change apparent speed and continuity even before RL, so it needs a separate ablation.
Evaluation should report SFT stability/safety first, SFT action-frequency effects second, then RL improvement in speed, acceleration profile, jerk, success rate, and force / tactile overshoot.
The most important research question is which operation types can be accelerated without losing physical reliability.
